title: Air-fryer chicken wings
subtitle: Crisp wings with a dry-brine overnight trick
excerpt: Chicken wings dry-brined overnight with salt and a small amount of baking powder, then cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 22 minutes. The baking powder draws out moisture and produces an exceptionally crisp skin that rivals deep-fried without any oil. Serve plain or toss in a sauce.
sourceNotes: Crispy chicken wings without deep-frying became a key test of air-fryer capability when the appliance became widespread. The baking powder trick was developed by American cooks and popularised by Kenji López-Alt. Baking powder (not soda) is alkaline and promotes the Maillard reaction on the skin, producing a brown, crackled exterior that mimics deep-frying. The technique works because it draws moisture out of the skin during the overnight rest.

content.text: The overnight rest is the most important step. The salt and baking powder mixture draws moisture out of the skin, which then dries on a rack in the fridge overnight. By the time the wings go into the air fryer they are surface-dry, and the result is a shatteringly crisp skin. Wings cooked without this step are good; wings cooked with it are exceptional.

items.name: Chicken wings
items.prepNote: split at the joint if whole

content.text: Pat the {{chicken-wings}} thoroughly dry with kitchen paper. Toss with the {{sea-salt-fine}}, {{baking-powder}}, and {{black-pepper}} until every wing is coated. Arrange on a wire rack set over a baking tray. Refrigerate uncovered for at least 8 hours, or overnight. When ready to cook, preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Arrange the wings in a single layer in the basket; cook in two batches if needed. Cook at 200°C for 22 minutes, flipping once at 11 minutes, until deep golden and the skin is crisp and crackled. Serve immediately.

items.fix: The wings must be completely dry before adding the baking powder. Any surface moisture prevents the crisping. Leave uncovered on the rack in the fridge; covered wings steam slightly and stay moist
items.cause: Wings were not patted dry before the baking powder was added, or the fridge was too humid
items.symptom: Wings not crispy despite the overnight rest

items.fix: Use aluminium-free baking powder. Most supermarkets stock it. Standard baking powder often contains added aluminium.
items.cause: Aluminium-based baking powder leaves a metallic note
items.symptom: Slight bitter taste on the skin

items.fix: Add 3 minutes on high at the end. Alternatively, broil for 2 minutes under the grill after air frying: this finishes the colour without overcooking the meat
items.cause: Air fryer runs cool, or wings were wet when they went in
items.symptom: Wings cooked through but skin not very dark

content.text: Plain air-fryer wings are the base for every wing recipe in this section. Serve as they are with blue cheese dip and celery, or toss hot from the basket into buffalo sauce, BBQ sauce, or honey and garlic. The overnight prep is the only inconvenience: the cook time is 22 minutes flat.
